content:
# Tight Gas Reservoirs

**Summary**: Reservoirs with low permeability that require special completion techniques.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014]] investigated the Sarah Formation as a target for tight-gas-reservoirs.

- [[2016-ejaz|Ejaz, 2016]] studied tight-gas-reservoirs in the Sarah Formation to optimize development strategies.

- [[2017-alqubalee|Alqubalee, 2017]] investigated the Sarah Formation as a potential tight gas reservoir.

- [[2020-islam|Islam, 2020]] evaluated the Sarah Formation as a target tight gas reservoir.

- [[2024-mohamed|Mohamed, 2024]] evaluated flowback additives specifically for tight gas formations.

## Key Subtopics

- [[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014]] ['porosity', 'permeability', 'diagenesis']

- [[2016-ejaz|Ejaz, 2016]] studied tight-gas-reservoirs in the Sarah Formation to optimize development strategies.

- [[2017-alqubalee|Alqubalee, 2017]] investigated the Sarah Formation as a potential tight gas reservoir.

- [[2020-islam|Islam, 2020]] evaluated the Sarah Formation as a target tight gas reservoir.

- [[2024-mohamed|Mohamed, 2024]] evaluated flowback additives specifically for tight gas formations.

## Methods and Approaches Used

- [[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014]] ['thin-section-petrography', 'mercury-injection-capillary-pressure']

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The Sarah Formation is considered a primary reservoir target for tight gas in Saudi Arabia. ([[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014, p. 19]])

- The Sarah Formation is a potential tight gas reservoir characterized by low porosity and very low permeability. ([[2016-ejaz|Ejaz, 2016, p. 13]])

- The formation is characterized by glaciogenic features and heterogeneous lithofacies. ([[2017-alqubalee|Alqubalee, 2017, p. 17]])

- Defined by Saudi Aramco standards as having porosity < 12% and permeability < 1 mD. ([[2020-islam|Islam, 2020, p. 39]])

- Ionic liquids are promising candidates for improving production in tight gas formations due to their stability. ([[2024-mohamed|Mohamed, 2024, p. 21]])

## Competing Approaches and Debates

- [[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014]] Predicting reservoir quality in complex facies and environments.
